The `split('=')` splits on every `=` character. For `'url=http://x=y'`, this produces `['url', 'http', 'x', 'y']` which can't be unpacked into two variables. The fix is to split only on the **first** `=` using `split('=', 1)`.
Need to split only on the FIRST equals sign to preserve any `=` characters in the value. Use `split('=', 1)` to limit to one split.

The split without `maxsplit` splits on ALL `=` characters. For values containing `=` (like URLs), this produces more than 2 parts, causing unpacking to fail. The correct approach is to use `maxsplit=1` to only split on the first `=`.
Need to split on `=` only once (from the left) using `split('=', 1)` to preserve any `=` characters in the value portion.
When splitting by `=`, we need to split only on the FIRST equals sign, not all of them. Using `split('=', 1)` will fix the URL case.
